⭐ Health and Physical Education

Clay Work

Create beautiful art with clay, wood, and paper!

📚 Unit 11 ⏱️ 9 min read

Unit 11: Clay Work

Clay work and paper crafts are fun ways to create three-dimensional art. This unit covers wood print, clay modeling, and paper weaving.

Lesson 1: Wood Print

What is Wood Print?

Wood print (also called woodblock printing) is a technique where a design is carved into a piece of wood, then ink or paint is applied, and it is pressed onto paper or fabric.

Materials Needed for Wood Print

MaterialUse
Soft wood block (pine, plywood)Surface for carving
Carving tools (gouges, chisels)Cutting the design
PencilDrawing design
Carbon paperTransferring design
Printing ink or poster colorColoring the block
Roller (brayer)Applying ink evenly
Paper or fabricPrinting surface
Spoon or pressApplying pressure

Steps for Wood Print

StepAction
1Draw a simple design on paper
2Transfer the design to the wood block
3Carve away the areas you do NOT want to print
4The raised areas will print (relief printing)
5Apply ink or color evenly with a roller
6Place paper on top of the inked block
7Press firmly with a spoon or press
8Carefully peel the paper off
9Let it dry

Simple Designs for Beginners

DesignDifficulty
LeafEasy
StarEasy
FishMedium
FlowerMedium
BirdMedium

Safety Tips for Wood Print

Safety ruleWhy
Always carve away from your bodyPrevents cuts
Use a carving boardProtects the table
Keep tools sharpDull tools slip more
Ask an adult for helpCarving tools are sharp
Do not rushCarving takes patience

Lesson 2: Clay Work

What is Clay?

Clay is a natural material made of fine soil particles. When mixed with water, it becomes soft and moldable. When dried or baked, it becomes hard.

Types of Clay

TypeDescriptionBest for
Natural clay (terracotta)Found in soil, needs firingPots, sculptures
Air-dry clayDries without bakingClassroom projects
Polymer clayBaked in ovenSmall, detailed objects
Play doughSoft, colorfulYoung children

Tools for Clay Work

ToolUse
Rolling pinFlatten clay
Clay knifeCut clay
Needle toolDetails, poking holes
SpongeSmooth surface with water
Wire cutterCutting large clay blocks
Modeling standHold clay while working

Basic Clay Techniques

1. Pinching

StepAction
1Roll clay into a ball
2Push thumb into the center
3Pinch walls evenly with fingers
4Smooth inside and outside

Makes: Small bowls, cups

2. Coiling

StepAction
1Roll clay into long snakes (coils)
2Stack coils in a circle
3Blend coils together
4Smooth inside and outside

Makes: Pots, vases, baskets

3. Slab Building

StepAction
1Roll clay flat (like a cookie)
2Cut shapes from the slab
3Join pieces with slip (watery clay)
4Smooth seams

Makes: Boxes, houses, tiles

4. Sculpting

StepAction
1Build a basic shape
2Add clay for details
3Remove clay to refine
4Smooth and finish

Makes: Animals, people, figures

Simple Clay Projects for Class 5

ProjectTechniqueSteps
Clay bowlPinchingBall → press thumb → pinch walls
Clay potCoilingMake coils → stack → smooth
Clay animalSculptingBody → legs → head → details
Clay beadsRollingRoll small balls → poke hole → dry
Leaf imprintPressingRoll flat → press real leaf → remove leaf

Drying and Finishing Clay

StageWhat happensTime
Leather hardClay is firm but can be carved1-3 hours
Bone dryCompletely dry, ready for firing1-7 days
Bisque firedFirst firing (in kiln)Several hours
GlazedGlass-like coating appliedAfter bisque

(For classroom without kiln, use air-dry clay.)

Decorating Clay

MethodHow to do it
PaintingUse acrylic or poster colors after clay is dry
GlazingApply glaze and fire in kiln
EngravingCarve designs into leather-hard clay
StampingPress objects into soft clay

Lesson 3: Paper Weaving Art

What is Paper Weaving?

Paper weaving is a craft where strips of paper are woven together (over and under) to create a patterned design.

Materials for Paper Weaving

MaterialUse
Colored paper (2-3 colors)Strips for weaving
ScissorsCutting paper
RulerMeasuring strips
PencilMarking lines
GlueSecuring ends
Cardboard (for loom)Base for weaving

Steps for Paper Weaving

StepAction
1Choose a base paper (loom)
2Fold it in half
3Cut slits from the fold (not through the edges)
4Open the paper – you have a warp (vertical strips)
5Cut strips of contrasting paper (weft – horizontal strips)
6Weave: over the first warp, under the second, over the third…
7Push each strip up tightly
8Glue the ends at the back
9Trim edges neatly

Weaving Pattern: Over-Under

RowPattern
Row 1Over, under, over, under…
Row 2Under, over, under, over…
Row 3Same as Row 1
Row 4Same as Row 2

💡 Tip: Alternate the starting pattern each row to get a checkerboard effect!

Paper Weaving Variations

VariationHow to do it
CheckerboardTwo colors, alternate over-under
StripedUse multiple colors for strips
Diagonal weaveCut slits at an angle
Curved weaveCut curved warp strips
Photo weavingWeave strips of a photograph

What to Make with Paper Weaving

ProjectDescription
PlacematWoven paper mat for table
Greeting cardWeave on the front of a card
BookmarkSmall woven strip
Wall hangingLarger weaving with fringe
BasketWeave around a container

Try It Yourself!

Exercise 1: Fill in the Blanks

  1. In wood printing, the raised areas are the parts that ______.
  2. ______ clay dries without needing a kiln.
  3. Rolling clay into long snakes is called the ______ technique.
  4. In paper weaving, the vertical strips are called the ______.
  5. The horizontal strips in weaving are called the ______.

Exercise 2: True or False

  1. Wood printing uses a soft wood block. (T/F)
  2. Pinching is a clay technique for making bowls. (T/F)
  3. Paper weaving uses only one color of paper. (T/F)
  4. Air-dry clay needs to be fired in a kiln. (T/F)
  5. In weaving, “over-under” means one strip goes over, one under. (T/F)

Exercise 3: Match the Clay Technique to Its Product

TechniqueProduct
Pinchinga) Pot from stacked coils
Coilingb) Flat tile
Slabc) Bowl from thumb press
Sculptingd) Animal figure

Exercise 4: Name the Craft

Read the description and name the craft.

  1. Carving a design into wood and printing it on paper → ______
  2. Rolling clay into snakes and stacking them → ______
  3. Putting paper strips over and under each other → ______

Answer Key

Exercise 1: 1. print, 2. Air-dry, 3. coiling, 4. warp, 5. weft

Exercise 2: 1. T, 2. T, 3. F, 4. F, 5. T

Exercise 3: Pinching→c, Coiling→a, Slab→b, Sculpting→d

Exercise 4: 1. Wood print, 2. Coiling, 3. Paper weaving

Great work! Your hands can create amazing things from clay, wood, and paper. Keep crafting!
